title: Pointer Gap At Resume
learning_objective: Trace a resume event where optimizer_step lags global_step after a checkpoint reload and decide which counter should be trusted first.
anti_template_note: Explains how to reconcile counters using concrete checkpoint fields and log lines instead of generic resume advice.

title: Best Or Latest Pointer Choice
learning_objective: Diagnose whether best.ckpt or latest.ckpt is safe to inspect when summary.json and directory timestamps disagree.
anti_template_note: Anchors the lesson to competing artifacts rather than abstract checkpoint terminology.

title: Partial Save Missing Optimizer State
learning_objective: Recognize the artifact pattern of a checkpoint save that wrote model weights but missed optimizer shards.
anti_template_note: Uses a broken directory example instead of generic warnings about incomplete saves.

title: Stale Best Step After Resume
learning_objective: Show how best_step can stay stale across resume while latest_step advances correctly in logs.
anti_template_note: Pairs a stale field with an updated log to teach field trust ranking.

title: Shard Count Mismatch Review
learning_objective: Review a multi-shard checkpoint whose metadata claims four shards while the directory only contains three.
anti_template_note: Makes the learner count real artifact pieces rather than read policy prose.

title: Device Remap Warning Read
learning_objective: Interpret a reload log that silently remaps optimizer tensors from cuda:3 to cuda:0 and identify the safe follow-up check.
anti_template_note: Centers on a concrete warning message and the artifact it implicates.

title: Restart Seed Drift
learning_objective: Explain why validation divergence can come from restart-time dataloader seed drift even when the checkpoint itself is intact.
anti_template_note: Links a config seed field to observable validation behavior.

title: Cadence Overlap Comparison
learning_objective: Compare two runs where checkpoint save cadence and eval cadence collide and produce confusing step ownership in logs.
anti_template_note: Uses timeline comparison rather than generic scheduling notes.

title: Directory Name Hides Gap
learning_objective: Spot a checkpoint directory name that looks monotonic even though step_12400 was never written.
anti_template_note: Forces comparison between names and counters instead of trusting strings.

title: Resume Loss Jump Triage
learning_objective: Differentiate a post-resume loss jump caused by scheduler state reset from one caused by corrupted model weights.
anti_template_note: Teaches differential diagnosis across multiple runtime signals.

title: Tensor Shape Regression Guard
learning_objective: Verify that a resumed embedding table shape matches the new tokenizer size before trusting a successful load message.
anti_template_note: Grounds the lesson in an actual tensor shape check.

title: Reload Roundtrip Logit Drift
learning_objective: Compare logits before and after a reload to decide whether apparent drift is within rounding noise or indicates a wrong state restore.
anti_template_note: Uses concrete numbers instead of generic correctness language.

title: Allocated Versus Reserved On A100
learning_objective: Interpret a run where allocated memory stays near 39 GB while reserved memory climbs toward 71 GB on an 80 GB A100.
anti_template_note: Anchors memory reasoning to explicit A100 numbers over time.

title: Validation Only Memory Spike

title: Microbatch Pushes Past Budget
learning_objective: Use a numeric budget sheet to explain why increasing microbatch from 4 to 5 causes OOM at seq_len 4096.

title: Fragmentation Or Real Leak

title: Pinned Host Buffer Backpressure
learning_objective: Explain a GPU memory incident that starts with host-side pinning pressure before the allocator reports stress.

title: Attention Mask Expansion Cost

title: Accumulation Plateau Memory Pattern
